hypothesis: Integrating replica-exchange Hamiltonian Monte Carlo across three auxiliary temperatures (β = 1.0, 0.5, 0.25) during AMDEN denoising for t ∈ [0, 0.6] will reproduce the annealed-Si structural/energy distributions and yield target-aligned Young’s moduli on MEG (regression slope ≈ 1, R² ≥ 0.95) with at least 40% fewer HMC integration steps than the current single-temperature scheme.
